LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DLA PIPER LLP (US) PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION GRANTED. THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT IS A SEPARATE AGREEMENT APPLYING TO ISSUES OF TERMINATION. THE PARTIES CLEARLY ENTERED INTO AN ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IN THE EMPLOYMENT OFFER LETTER. THERE IS A PRESUMPTION IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION, AND ANY DOUBTS CONCERNING THE SCOPE OF ARBITRAL ISSUES SHOULD BE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION. SEE CIONE VS. FORESTERS EQUITY SERVICES, INC. (1997) 58 CAL.APP. 4TH 625.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF M. JENKS' DEMURRER TO PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS OVERRULED. A DEMURRER IS NOT A PROPER RESPONSE TO A PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT HAS STANDING TO BRING THE PETITION AS THE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS REQUESTING FOR A STATEMENT OF DECISION TO BE ISSUED BY THE COURT PURSUANT TO CCP 632 AND CCP 1291. JUDGE: ERNEST H. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION / Notice Of Hearing On New Trial Motion
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL RUBIN OF THE ALTSHULER BERZON LLP, TEL. NO. 421-7151 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER T. SCANLAN OF ARNOLD & PORTER LLP, TEL. NO. 471-3167 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DLA PIPER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF M. TODD JENKS' MOTION For A New Trial is Denied. Plaintiff has not established a basis for a new trial under CCP sec. 657. Plaintiff contends that the Court erred in finding that DLA Piper could enforce the arbitration provision in Plaintiff's offer letter. Plaintiff did not raise this argument in its opposition to Defendant's motion to compel arbitration. (Declaration of Christopher Scanlan, Ex. A, Jenk's Response to DLA Piper's Petition to Compel Arbitration.) Moreover, even if this argument had been raised, the Court would not have erred in finding that DLA Piper was entitled to enforce the agreement pursuant to Corp. Code sec. 16914(a). Plaintiff next contends that the Court erred in finding that his claims were within the scope of the arbitration agreement. Plaintiff argues that his claims arise under the Termination Agreement and 2006 STD Plan, which do not contain arbitration provisions. Plaintiff failed to provide a copy of these agreements as evidence in support of this motion. Further, the Termination Agreement has a limited integration clause and therefore does not supersede the arbitration provision in the employment offer. (See Cione v. Foresters Equity Servs., Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 645, 637-38.) The arbitrator held that the 2006 STD Plan was not a contract. (Declaration of Christopher Scanlan, Ex. E, Arbitration Award, p. 6., "An STD claim could under certain circumstances be a contract. I hold that in this situation it was not.") Plaintiff contends that the Court erred in finding that it could not reconsider the arbitrability challenges raised in its opposition to the motion to confirm the arbitration award. "The court may not confirm an award without first finding the parties agreed in writing to arbitrate their dispute, unless a judicial determination of the issue has already been made (e.g., by a court considering a petition to compel arbitration)." (Toal v. Tardiff (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1208, 1220.) Because the Court determined the issue of arbitrability when it granted the motion to compel arbitration, the Court did not err in not reconsidering the issue in connection with confirming the arbitration award. (See CCP sec. 1008(e).) Plaintiff contends that the Court erred in concluding that the Court lacked jurisdiction to vacate or modify the arbitration award because Plaintiff failed to timely file a petition to vacate or modify the award. CCP sec. 1286 provides that "[i]f a petition or response under this chapter is duly served and filed, the court shall confirm the award as made, whether rendered in this state or another state, unless in accordance with this chapter it corrects the award and confirms it as corrected, vacates the award or dismisses the proceeding." Because there was no timely petition to vacate or modify the award, the mandatory language of CCP sec. 1286 required the Court to confirm the award. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award;
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD JOHNSTON OF THE JOHNSTON LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. (707) 577-7422 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF WITH THE PLAINTIFF MR. JENKS;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER T. SCANLAN OF ARNOLD & PORTER LLP, TEL. NO. 471-3167 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF M. JENKS' Demurrer To Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is overruled. A demurrer is not a proper response to a petition to confirm arbitration award. In addition, defendant has standing to bring the petition as the successor in interest. Plaintiff's counsel is requesting for a Statement of Decision to be issued by the court pursuant to CCP 632 AND CCP 1291. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD JOHNSTON OF THE JOHNSTON LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. (707) 577-7422 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF WITH THE PLAINTIFF MR. JENKS;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER T. SCANLAN OF ARNOLD & PORTER LLP, TEL. NO. 471-3167 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF M. JENKS's Motion For Order Staying Proceedings On Defendant Dla Piper'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is denied. A stay is not warranted. This Court was the first to assert jurisdiction over the subject matter. See Mave Enterprises, Inc. v. Travelers Indemnity Co. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1408. Plaintiff's counsel is requesting for a Statement of Decision to be issued by the court pursuant to CCP 632 AND CCP 1291. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration; Points And Authorities , Declarations Of David J. Reis And Lisa Partridge In Support
Off Calendar Apr-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DLA PIPER LLP (US) Petition to Compel Arbitration. Appearances: David J. Reis, Esq. of Howard Rice (415) 434-1600 appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Richard Johnston, Esq. Attorney at Law, (707) 577-7422 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition to compel arbitration granted. The Termination agreement is a separate agreement applying to issues of termination. The parties clearly entered into an arbitration agreement in the employment offer letter. There is a presumption in favor of arbitration, and any doubts concerning the scope of arbitral issues should be resolved in favor of arbitration. See Cione vs. Foresters Equity Services, Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App. 4th 625.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
